message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class. Need to touch _termui_impl.py and termui.py. What files does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing multi-file support for click.edit(). I need to own _termui_impl.py and termui.py. Please propose your files.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: We both need the same 2 files - true disjoint split isn't possible. Propose sequential handoff instead: you edit first for multi-file support, send DONE, then I layer process_priority on top and send my own DONE. I'll wait for your DONE before touching anything. OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
your_files: src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both touch these 2 files, so full overlap isn't possible - let's split by file instead. I'll own _termui_impl.py: I rename Editor.edit_file->edit_files, make it accept a list of filenames, and update the subprocess call to pass multiple quoted filenames. You own termui.py and can add your process_priority param there AND to Editor.__init__ call site - just don't touch Editor internals in _termui_impl.py directly; instead tell me what Editor.__init__/edit signature changes you need (e.g. process_priority: int|None) and I'll wire them into _termui_impl.py so we don't clobber each other. For my part, in termui.py I need: edit()'s filename param typed as 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None', and it just needs to be forwarded as-is to Editor().edit(filename) - I'll leave termui.py to you, just please keep that filename type change and forwarding intact when you add process_priority. Let me know if this works.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed - sequential handoff. I'll implement multi-file edit support now in both files and send DONE when finished. Please wait until then before editing. I'll keep changes minimal/additive so your process_priority layering is easy.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
note: Agreed on termui.py being mine outright, and I'll keep the filename type as 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None' and forward it as-is when adding process_priority. For _termui_impl.py: rather than you wiring in my process_priority logic (risk of me not being able to verify it), let's do sequential edits on that one file - you finish your edit_files rename/multi-file changes first and send DONE, then I'll layer in the process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and the priority-setting logic in edit()/edit_file(s) afterward, touching only what's needed and not reverting your rename. I won't touch _termui_impl.py until I see your DONE. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Confirmed - sequential handoff. Waiting for your DONE before I touch either file.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file edit support. Summary of changes so you can layer on top cleanly: (1) In _termui_impl.py, Editor.edit_file(filename: str) was renamed to Editor.edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) - it now joins quoted filenames and passes them all to the editor subprocess in one call; Editor.edit() internally calls self.edit_files([name]) instead of self.edit_file(name). (2) In termui.py, the public edit() function's filename param is now typed 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None', with two @t.overload signatures added above the implementation, and the body normalizes filename into a list before calling ed.edit_files(filenames). For your process_priority param: add it to Editor.__init__ and to the subprocess.Popen call in edit_files (e.g. via preexec_fn/nice or Windows priority flags), and thread a matching process_priority kwarg through termui.py's edit() call site into Editor(...). Should be a straightforward additive layer - let me know if you hit any conflicts. My patch is written to patch.txt already, so feel free to git diff to see current state before you start editing.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Confirming - I already sent DONE (may have crossed with your last messages). My multi-file edit implementation is complete in both files and patch.txt is written. You're clear to start layering process_priority now - go ahead and touch both files as needed for your feature.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished layering process_priority on top. Added Editor.__init__(process_priority=None), a private _get_popen_kwargs() helper that maps 'low'/'below_normal'/'normal'/'above_normal'/'high' to os.nice() values (preexec_fn) on POSIX or subprocess priority-class creationflags on Windows, invalid values silently ignored (fall back to None), and edit_file() falls back to a plain Popen call if applying the priority flags itself raises OSError. termui.py's edit() gained a process_priority passthrough param and docstring entry. Note: my sandbox's repo was pristine (no trace of your _termui_impl.py/termui.py edits), so I implemented against the original code rather than your renamed edit_files - each of us apparently writes patch.txt against our own isolated copy, so no file clobbering actually occurred. My patch.txt is written.
